name: hein-hplc

metadata.name: hein-hplc
metadata.summary: This web app analyze deconvoluted HPLC peaks and plot time course data

documentation:
# Hein HPLC - peak deconvolution and time course plot

## Description
This Dash web app aims to perform peak deconvolution without peak assignment or manual baseline correction. This app is developed by mainly referencing HPLC chromatograph. However, by modifying the default peak detection width, this app should be compatible with general peak analysis. It is also capable of processing multiple HPLC data and generating peak area over sample profile in csv.


## Installation - pip
```
pip install hein-hplc
```

## Usage
Run the app.py or use the command below. Click the url displayed in Terminal (default: [http://localhost:8050](http://localhost:8050)), upload .csv or .UV file to get started. Ctrl+C to stop the app.
```
hein-hplc
```


## Data format
### Chemstattion .UV
Support chemstation .UV file
### CSV
For data in .csv, column names are not required. For time course plot (example in [sample_data.csv](sample_data/sample_data.csv)), the column name should be TIME (HH:MM:SS).
You can append as many as columns with the same retention time scales.

| Retention Time minutes | 10:39:56 | 10:55:59 | 11:11:43 | ... |
|------------------------|----------|----------|----------|-----|
| 0.00125                | 0.42767  | 0.09733  | 0.02217  | ... |
| 0.00292                | 0.41933  | 0.086    | 0.01417  | ... |
| 0.00458                | ...      | ...      | ...      | ... |

functions.name: hein_hplc.app.update_widget
documentation: when file is uploaded, update the dropdown menu and retention time range accordingly

functions.name: hein_hplc.app.update_plot
documentation: Generate your plot based on the widget inputs

functions.name: hein_hplc.peak_analysis.peak_deconvolution.find_baseline
documentation: find baselines according to first derivative of absorbance

functions.name: hein_hplc.peak_analysis.peak_deconvolution.find_para
documentation: find peak fit parameters and boundaries

functions.name: hein_hplc.peak_analysis.peak_deconvolution.group_items
documentation: group peaks between data if they are within a certain retention time

functions.name: hein_hplc.peak_analysis.peak_deconvolution.format_save_csv
documentation: save time course data to csv

functions.name: hein_hplc.peak_analysis.peak_deconvolution.second_derivative
documentation: find second derivative with option to smooth both derivatives

functions.name: hein_hplc.peak_analysis.peak_deconvolution.find_peak_group
documentation:
auto-detect baseline and peak group range:
1. find baseline: plateaus regions using first derivative (find small down peaks)
2. find peak_group_ranges:
    a. preliminary peak finding: prominence=(1, None)
    b. start, end for all peaks (looking for the closest baseline point)
    c. remove duplicate ranges

methods.name: hein_hplc.peak_analysis.peak_analysis_main.PeakAnalysis.peak_deconvolution
documentation:
STEP 1: pre-process data:
1. pre_process_data: remove data points if there are more than 2400 data point / min
2. select_range: select the interest range
3. dead_volume: ignore data before dead_volume
4. normalized_y: normalize absorbance using y_max to 100

STEP 2: find baseline and peak group range
1. find baseline: plateaus regions using first derivative (find small down peaks)
2. find peak_group_ranges:
    a. preliminary peak finding: prominence=(1, None)
    b. start, end for all peaks (looking for the closest baseline point)
    c. remove duplicate ranges

STEP 3: find peak
1. find from original HPLC or second derivative
2. remove peaks before dead volume

STEP 4: curve fit for each peak group
for group in peak groups
    1. find initial parameters and boundaries
    2. fit curve
    3. find retention time
    4. calculate peak area
    5. calculate tailing factor, ratio at 1/20 height above baseline

methods.name: hein_hplc.peak_analysis.peak_analysis_main.PeakAnalysis.pre_process_data
documentation: trim data if sampling rate is higher than 2400 point per min

methods.name: hein_hplc.peak_analysis.peak_analysis_main.PeakAnalysis.pre_process_x
documentation: trim time data if sampling rate is higher than 2400 point per min
